name: Quick Cranberry Cobbler
recipeIngredient: 1 package Biscuit dough to make 8 bis.
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate the orange rind.
2. Juice the orange.
3. Combine orange juice, grated rind, water, sugar replacement, cranberries, and butter in a saucepan.
4. Bring mixture to a boil and cook for 1 minute.
5. Spoon cranberry sauce evenly into 8 well-greased baking dishes or a casserole dish.
6. Top the sauce with biscuit dough.
7. Bake at 450°F for 10 minutes, then reduce heat to 350°F and bake for an additional 20 minutes.
Serving size: 8 servings
Calories per serving: 98
Nutritional content: 1 bread, 3/4 fruit
Source: Diabetic High Fiber Cookbook by Mary Jane Finsand, c. 1985. Shared but not tested by Elizabeth Rodier, Oct 1993.
Note: Try using half the recipe with 1/2 teaspoon dried orange peel and 3 or 4 tablespoons unsweetened orange juice. Simply cook the cranberry mixture in a saucepan and serve with turkey. Using half the recipe without biscuits would result in about 3 fruit exchanges.
